tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mild hypoglycemia, elevated serum creatinine suggesting possible renal impairment, and increased HbA1c indicating suboptimal glycemic control. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), which may suggest renal dysfunction and possible urinary tract pathology. Recommend clinical correlation for renal and metabolic status, and further evaluation for underlying causes.

display.value: Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
